faq.question: How much should elastic be cut?
faq.answer: Cut elastic to 75-90% of the body measurement, depending on application. Waistband: 85%. Cuffs: 80%. These are starting points — test on scrap first, as elastic stretch varies widely by brand and width.

faq.question: Which elastic width should I use?
faq.answer: 1/4": necklines, sleeve gathering. 1/2": lightweight garments, cuffs. 3/4"-1": standard waistbands. 1.5"-2": heavy-duty waistbands, pajamas. Wider elastic distributes pressure and is more comfortable for waistbands.

faq.question: Why does my elastic twist in the casing?
faq.answer: Common causes: elastic casing too wide (should be elastic width + 1/4"), elastic not tacked at seam lines, or using braided elastic (which narrows when stretched and twists). Use knit or woven elastic for casings — it maintains width when stretched.
